title: Out-of-Distribution Detection and Selective Generation for Conditional Language Models

structured.summary: The paper proposes lightweight Mahalanobis distance-based out-of-distribution (OOD) detection methods leveraging encoder-decoder embeddings for conditional language models, showing improved OOD detection and selective generation performance over perplexity and other baselines on summarization and translation tasks. The approach combines in-domain and background Gaussian fits to derive relative scores that correlate well with quality and enable abstention from low-confidence outputs.

strengths.point: Effective and lightweight embedding-based OOD detection
strengths.evidence: The method uses simple Gaussian fits to encoder and decoder embeddings with Mahalanobis distances, achieving strong empirical OOD detection performance that surpasses perplexity and other embedding or likelihood baselines on XSUM summarization and WMT15 En-Fr translation.

strengths.point: Improved selective generation via combination with perplexity
strengths.evidence: Combining the proposed relative Mahalanobis distance scores with perplexity yields better correlation with human and automatic quality metrics across in-domain and OOD data, enabling reliable abstention and improved average generation quality without requiring additional training.

weaknesses.point: Lack of comparison with recent generative-model-specific OOD detection methods
weaknesses.evidence: The paper omits comparison or discussion of recent likelihood-ratio, energy-based, and ensemble uncertainty OOD methods tailored for generative models, limiting understanding of novelty and relative advantage.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient empirical justification and analysis of key assumptions
weaknesses.evidence: No evidence is provided that the high-dimensional embeddings follow Gaussian distributions; no normality tests or alternative density models are explored. Pooling strategy (simple averaging) is neither justified nor ablated.

weaknesses.point: Reproducibility concerns due to missing experimental and implementation details
weaknesses.evidence: Critical details such as training protocols for logistic regression, Gaussian fitting parameters, dataset sampling and preprocessing for background Gaussians, and score combination methods for selective generation are not disclosed.

weaknesses.point: Limited evaluation scope and baseline coverage
weaknesses.evidence: Experiments are restricted to English summarization and translation with no multilingual or other conditional generation tasks; selective generation baselines exclude recent quality estimation models; linguistic or statistical characterization of OOD splits is missing.

questions.question: How sensitive is the method to the Gaussian assumption on embeddings and the choice of pooling strategy?
questions.motivation: Validating these assumptions is critical to ensure the robustness and generalizability of the Mahalanobis distance-based scores.

questions.question: How does the proposed approach compare quantitatively to recent likelihood-ratio or energy-based OOD detection methods specific to generative models?
questions.motivation: Understanding relative performance against state-of-the-art alternatives is important for assessing the novelty and practical utility.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of comparison with recent generative-model-specific OOD detection methods. The paper omits comparison or discussion of recent likelihood-ratio, energy-based, and ensemble uncertainty OOD methods tailored for generative models, limiting understanding of novelty and relative advantage
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient empirical justification and analysis of key assumptions. No evidence is provided that the high-dimensional embeddings follow Gaussian distributions; no normality tests or alternative density models are explored. Pooling strategy (simple averaging) is neither justified nor ablated
critique_points.point_003: Reproducibility concerns due to missing experimental and implementation details. Critical details such as training protocols for logistic regression, Gaussian fitting parameters, dataset sampling and preprocessing for background Gaussians, and score combination methods for selective generation are not disclosed
critique_points.point_004: Limited evaluation scope and baseline coverage. Experiments are restricted to English summarization and translation with no multilingual or other conditional generation tasks; selective generation baselines exclude recent quality estimation models; linguistic or statistical characterization of OOD splits is missing
